authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 453 - Funeral Industry Practices
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR publishes the controlling federal Funeral Rule text for funeral-provider disclosures, itemized statement, embalming representations, required-purchase limits, services without prior approval, and document retention. These controls determine when funeral-home operations can progress or close.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: price-list disclosure, selected-services package, services-without-prior-approval control, document retention, and completion-evidence review
used_for: completion refusal when disclosure or retention evidence is missing

authoritative_sources.title: Complying with the Funeral Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the federal Funeral Rule regulator. This guidance is directly written for funeral providers and translates the rule into practical price-list, telephone price, selection statement, disclosure, embalming, and retention workflows that funeral-home operations must track.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: case intake, price discussion dependency tracking, goods and services selection, itemized statement readiness, and disclosure exception handling
used_for: operations handoff evidence from arrangements to fulfillment

authoritative_sources.title: Funeral Directors' Handbook on Death Registration and Fetal Death Reporting
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, National Center for Health Statistics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC/NCHS publishes the national vital-statistics manual for funeral directors. It defines death-registration duties, demographic collection, medical-certifier coordination, registrar filing dependencies, and fetal-death handling that funeral-home operations must treat as evidence gates.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: death-certificate demographic intake, medical-certifier dependency tracking, registrar filing readiness, and disposition-permit readiness
used_for: blocked workflow handling when vital-record data is missing

authoritative_sources.title: Consumer Guide to Funeral and Cemetery Purchases
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Consumer Affairs Cemetery and Funeral Bureau
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: California's funeral and cemetery regulator publishes practical public guidance on right to control disposition, GPL/CPL and itemized statement expectations, embalming authorization, burial, cremation, hydrolysis, cemetery, licensing, and complaint pathways. It is authoritative for California and a useful example of state-level jurisdictional variance that this lane must surface rather than universalize.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: family authority validation, disposition path dependencies, cemetery or crematory coordination, embalming authorization boundary, and state-specific escalation
used_for: burial, cremation, hydrolysis, cemetery, and licensing terminology

authoritative_sources.title: Committal Service
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs, National Cemetery Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: VA National Cemetery Administration is the official source for national cemetery committal service constraints and burial-document expectations. It constrains funeral-home operations when a family requests veterans cemetery committal scheduling or eligibility confirmation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: veterans cemetery committal dependency review and adjacent-owner routing

authoritative_sources.title: Application of provisions of the Bloodborne Pathogens standard to Funeral and Nursing Homes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's official interpretation explains funeral-home obligations around human remains, occupational exposure, exposure incidents, and protective-equipment considerations. It is relevant to refusal triggers when a requester asks this lane to authorize body handling, preparation, PPE, or safety-critical actions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: remains handling, preparation, exposure-safety boundary, and care-center escalation

workflow_stages.name: Accepted intake and custody baseline
workflow_stages.description: Normalize first-call, decedent, location-of-remains, removal, transfer, custody, refrigeration, authorized arranger, person authorized to direct disposition, preneed instructions, PADD, power-of-attorney, and jurisdiction context before progressing operations.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Disclosure, selection, and arrangement handoff control
workflow_stages.description: Track GPL, Casket Price List, outer burial container price list, selected goods and services, cash-advance items, estimates, and itemized statement state as required evidence before operations treats services or merchandise as ready for fulfillment.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Care-center, service, and remains-readiness tracking
workflow_stages.description: Track refrigeration, embalming or declination posture, viewing or visitation request, care-center queue, merchandise, obituary, clergy, flowers, music, transportation, and service schedule dependencies without authorizing licensed preparation or safety decisions.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Vital-record, permit, and disposition-readiness tracking
workflow_stages.description: Track death-certificate demographic data, medical certifier or coroner status, eDRS filing reference, registrar dependency, burial transit or disposition permit, cemetery, crematory, hydrolysis, committal, interment-right, and destination confirmations as lifecycle blockers or next-owner tasks.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, rework, and adjacent-owner escalation
workflow_stages.description: Hold or reroute cases with missing custody evidence, disputed family authority, missing vital records, unsigned authorizations, care-center safety issues, cemetery or crematory conflicts, VA burial dependencies, payment exceptions, policy/source conflicts, or grief-support requests.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and audit-ready closure
workflow_stages.description: Close only when custody closure, required disclosures and selected-services records, authorization posture, vital-record references, permit posture, destination confirmations, service completion notes, owner handoffs, and audit trail entries are explicit.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: decedent case record, first-call note, removal or transfer record, chain-of-custody log, custody status, refrigeration or holding note, care-center work order, and location-of-remains record
authorized arranger or person authorized to direct disposition evidence, preneed contract, written instructions, advance directive, PADD, power-of-attorney references, and family-dispute notes
General Price List, Casket Price List, outer burial container price list, selected goods and services, cash-advance items, package estimate, itemized statement, and payment status
embalming authorization or declination, viewing or visitation request, preparation request, safety indicator, infectious-exposure note, and licensed funeral director, embalmer, or safety owner
death-certificate demographic intake, medical certifier status, coroner or medical examiner hold, burial transit or disposition permit, eDRS filing reference, and registrar dependency

service schedule, visitation, clergy or celebrant, obituary, music, flowers, casket, urn, merchandise, transportation, cemetery interment-right record, crematory or hydrolysis authorization, committal schedule, VA burial document reference, destination confirmation, and final disposition note
exception notes, rework checklists, owner-routing packages, completion evidence records, and audit trail entries
systems_of_record: family-service CRM or preneed management system
removal, transportation, or custody dispatch system
care-center, embalming-room, preparation-room, or remains-location log
electronic death-registration system or vital-record portal
cemetery management, interment-right, and committal scheduling system
service scheduling, obituary, merchandise, and chapel calendar systems
payment, POS, invoicing, trust, insurance, or preneed funding ledger
medical examiner, coroner, hospital release, hospice, nursing home, or certifier communication record
VA National Cemetery Administration scheduling or benefits reference
task, exception, compliance, safety, and audit workflow system

allowed: Classify funeral home operations requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request or validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, family-authority context, source context, systems-of-record references, and owner mappings required to proceed.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete lifecycle state based on documented custody, disclosure, selection, authorization, vital-record, service, destination, and next-owner evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed execution status updates, case or family-service workflow summaries, exception notes, completion records, and routing packages.
escalate: Custody, location-of-remains, authorized arranger, right-to-control disposition, preneed instruction, PADD, power-of-attorney, jurisdiction, or family-consensus facts are missing or disputed.
Death-certificate, medical certifier, coroner, disposition permit, cemetery, crematory, hydrolysis, service schedule, care-center, or VA burial documents are missing or materially conflict.

The request requires legal interpretation, licensed funeral directing or embalming judgment, care-center safety ownership, cemetery administration signoff, crematory authorization, benefits eligibility, payment approval, grief counseling, safety-critical action, or policy override.
Public sources, jurisdiction rules, cemetery or crematory requirements, tenant policy, preneed documents, or systems of record materially conflict.
refuse: Mark the operations case complete without custody closure, required disclosure and selection state, authorization posture, vital-record references, permit posture, destination confirmation, owner notes, and audit evidence.
Authorize embalming, body preparation, cremation, hydrolysis, burial, transportation, PPE, exposure response, or final disposition outside licensed or authorized owners.
Resolve legal family-authority disputes, approve financing or payment exceptions, adjudicate VA or insurance benefits, redesign arrangements, or provide grief, legal, clinical, or estate advice.

domain_failure_modes: the lane treats the funeral industry label as cosmetic and produces a generic operations update without custody, GPL, selected-services, authorization, death-registration, disposition, service, or destination evidence
a case is marked complete without chain-of-custody closure, itemized statement evidence, required price-list state, authorization posture, eDRS reference, permit status, or destination confirmation
family-authority or right-to-control disposition conflict is treated as an ordinary preference dispute instead of legal or compliance escalation
death certificate, medical certifier, coroner, eDRS, registrar, or disposition permit dependencies are skipped and downstream burial, cremation, or service scheduling fails
embalming, viewing, preparation, cremation, hydrolysis, transportation, or body-handling requests are authorized without licensed-owner, authorization, custody, or safety evidence
cemetery, crematory, VA, hydrolysis, green-burial, or destination requirements are overwritten by operations convenience instead of routed to the correct owner
tenant package-pricing, paid-in-full, pre-need, or payment rules are applied despite conflict with public source requirements or missing jurisdiction facts
grief support, estate advice, arrangement redesign, payment approval, or benefits eligibility is absorbed by operations because it arose in the same case queue

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Right-to-control disposition hierarchy, embalming authorization forms, cremation or hydrolysis authorization, disposition permits, cemetery regulation, care-center procedures, and preneed-funding rules vary materially by jurisdiction.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The validated pack treats jurisdiction, tenant policy, facility requirements, and licensed-owner authority as mandatory runtime facts. It does not guess outcomes where state, facility, or destination rules differ.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant systems may use different names for operations case records, custody logs, eDRS references, itemized statement artifacts, care-center work orders, and cemetery or crematory confirmations.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Runtime must retrieve tenant-specific system names and record locations before execution. The pack defines required source-of-truth classes and refusal behavior when mappings are missing.

notes: The research output constrains the final pack to funeral home operations evidence across custody, disclosure, vital-record, service, destination, exception, and closure workflows rather than generic work execution.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because all jurisdiction and tenant-specific variance is converted into explicit prerequisite, escalation, or refusal handling.
